primary.name: Alric (Anglo-Saxon)
primary.fact: Held Mattingley as alod (free tenure, no feudal lord but the king) of King Edward the Confessor. Anglo-Saxon noble. VCH Hants i, 505a. The earliest named individual associated with the place from which the Mattingley surname later derived. POSSIBLE distant social-line ancestor.

children.name: Alsi son of Brixi
children.fact: Norman-era Domesday tenant of Mattingley manor (TRW). Held under royal demesne. 8 villagers, 3 smallholders, 3 ploughlands, 1 mill (5s), fishery (100 eels). VCH Hants i, 505a. POSSIBLE social-line ancestor of the de Mattingley family.

children.name: Ellis (lord of Mattingley)
children.fact: First named lord of Mattingley village; recorded in Pipe Roll 13 Henry II (Pipe R. Soc. xi, 189)

children.name: Revelendus of Mattingley
children.fact: Succeeded Ellis; had 3 sons including Stephen

children.name: Stephen de Mattingley
children.fact: Earliest dated bearer of the Mattingley surname; granted mill to Prior of Merton

children.name: Peter de Mattingley
children.fact: Last lord; sold Mattingley manor to Geoffrey de Arundel

children.name: [~250-year documentary gap]
children.fact: Family likely yeoman farmers in Hampshire/Surrey/southern England — TNA subsidy rolls + Hampshire Record Office unindexed

children.name: Henry MATYNGLE
children.fact: Married 30 Sept 1548 Heckfield St Michael Hampshire (Phillimore's Transcript line 48); probable father of John Mattingly

children.name: John Mattingly
children.fact: Buried 17 Dec 1641 St Mary Colechurch London — primary source

children.name: Charles Mattingly
children.fact: Father of Thomas I; Sussex England

children.name: Thomas Mattingly I
children.fact: Born Sussex England; died there 1664 BEFORE emigrating; widow brought children to MD

children.name: Thomas Mattingly II
children.fact: THE MARYLAND IMMIGRANT — pre-1665; Mattingly's Hope land patent Sept 4 1666
children.spouse: m1. Mary Elizabeth Suttle (~1675); m2. Elizabeth Cole (~1690)

children.name: Elizabeth Mattingly
children.fact: Thomas II's first daughter

children.name: Judith Mattingly

children.name: Thomas Mattingly III
children.fact: Inherited Mount Misery plantation; will April 1774; m1. Elizabeth Warren, m2. Ruth Cole; 9 children
children.spouse: m1. Elizabeth Warren; m2. Ruth Cole

children.name: Edward Mattingly
children.fact: Son of Thomas III + Elizabeth Warren; Edward Mattingly is publicly documented as captain in the Upper Battalion of Militia in Saint Mary's County in 1779. The public role is confirmed; this exact placement in the Mount Misery branch remains probable.

children.name: Ignatius Mattingly
children.fact: Born Maryland; MD→KY migration; died there 1833. WikiTree Mattingly-140.

children.fact: WikiTree Mattingly-591; m1. Catherine Woodward (2 kids); m2. Elizabeth Christie (6 kids); KY→MO migration

children.name: John T. Mattingly

children.name: Edward Mattingly Sr.
children.fact: Born Missouri; migrated to TX by 1894; m. May Teichmueller 1 Sept 1894 (May d. 28 Aug 1900); m2. Blanche Schroeder
children.spouse: m1. May Teichmueller (1894–1900); m2. Blanche D. Schroeder (b. 1886 MO)

children.name: Leroy Teichmuller Mattingly
children.fact: Born La Grange TX; died San Antonio. U Texas engineer; named for German maternal grandfather Hans Teichmueller. m. Ruth Baity 4 Apr 1900–1982. WikiTree Mattingly-1127.

children.name: Leroy Baity Mattingly
children.fact: Born 17 May 1922 San Antonio; died Colorado Springs CO 2013. WikiTree Mattingly-1178. m. Jennive Imogene Lepick (1923-2008) at Fort Sill OK on 18 Dec 1943.
children.spouse: Jennive Imogene Lepick (2 Feb 1923 Floresville TX – 2008 Colorado Springs)

children.name: Sharyn ("Shari") Mattingly
children.fact: Family historian; Santa Monica; oil royalty owner

children.name: Rachel (née Trifon→Spence)
children.fact: Hunter's mother; daughter of Shari Mattingly + David Trifon (adopted-name Trifon). Married Dale William Spence Jr. — Hunter's surname comes via this marriage line, from Dale Sr's English Spence family.

children.name: Hunter Spence
children.fact: Recipient of this family history; recorded grandma's interview. Surname Spence inherited via FATHER (Dale William Spence Jr.), NOT via maternal grandfather David Trifon (whose Trifon surname was adopted, biological line untraceable).

children.name: Charmaine
children.fact: Shari + David Trifon's daughter; Hunter's aunt (maternal)

children.name: [brother]
children.fact: Got the chair from Pearl's set

children.name: Dr. Claude Mattingly
children.fact: Pediatrician; Marine WWI; suicide pact at Texan Hotel Austin Jan 31 1934

children.name: Aunt Mamie
children.fact: Mother died at her birth

children.name: Edward Mattingly Jr.
children.fact: Half-sibling — son of Edward Sr. + 2nd wife Blanche Schroeder

children.name: Leonard Mattingly Sr.
children.fact: Mattingly-62. 'Old Leonard'; one of the William/Leonard/Lucas brothers Webb places at Hardin's Creek in early 1786. m1. Mary Hayden; m2. Margaret Monarch; m3. Dorothy Hardesty; 10+ named children. SIBLING of Ignatius Jr (NOT direct ancestor of Hunter)

children.fact: Mattingly-339; son of Leonard Sr. Webb says Joseph Mattingly of Long Lick helped build the first Hardinsburg chapel by preparing heavy timbers and hand-sawing planks.

children.name: [Bardstown Mattingly bridge incomplete]
children.fact: Placeholder for a later Bardstown collateral line. Exact bridge from this Kentucky Mattingly cluster to Cyprian Peter Mattingly is still incomplete.

children.name: Marie Mattingly Meloney
children.fact: Major American journalist and editor, born Bardstown KY to physician Cyprian Peter Mattingly. Included only as a POSSIBLE Kentucky collateral until the branch bridge is documented.

children.fact: Mattingly-223. Co-recipient of Mattingly's Hope land patent 1666; m. Jane Suttle; brother of Hunter's direct ancestor Thomas II; Y-DNA R-Y14083 confirmed in his line (sibling terminal to Hunter's expected R-Y14084 cluster); ancestor of the Garrett County Hoyes settlement.

children.name: John Baptist Mattingly
children.fact: Mattingly-225; m. Grace Brewer; Cezar's son; great-grandfather of Henry I

children.fact: Son of Henry I; m. Nancy Ann Durbin; migrated to Missouri

children.name: Dominick Mattingly
children.fact: Anchor of Garrett County Mattingly settlement; bought 157 acres on Youghiogheny River from John Hoye 1831; the village was renamed 'Hoyes' in 1880

children.name: Meshack Mattingly
children.fact: Garrett County (MD) Commissioner; operated 'best stock farm in the county'; m. Mary P. Griffith (daughter of Pennsylvania Governor)

children.name: Rev. Romanus Mattingly
children.fact: Catholic priest; built parish churches at Oakland MD + Hoyes MD; 16-year pastor at Oakland

children.name: Judge Francis Mattingly
children.fact: Judge of the Orphans' Court at Cumberland MD (elected 1859); helped commit Allegany County to Union side 1861

secondary_trees.label: PATERNAL — Spence line (Dale Sr. Rice U professor, English immigrant → Beaumont TX)

tree.name: Dovie (?) Spence
tree.fact: Hunter's paternal great-grandmother; mother of Dr. Dale William Spence Sr. Maiden name UNKNOWN — research target.

children.name: Dr. Dale William Spence Sr.
children.fact: Hunter's paternal grandfather. Professor at Rice University, Houston. Immigrated from England (per Hunter's family tradition) to Beaumont, Texas. Patriarch of the Spence-Beaumont line. m. Alice Marie Henslee Spence.

children.name: Dale William Spence Jr.
children.fact: Hunter Spence's father. m. Rachel.

children.name: Hunter Spence
children.fact: Recipient of this family history; recorded grandma's interview. Surname Spence inherited via FATHER (Dale William Spence Jr.), NOT via maternal grandfather David Trifon (whose Trifon surname was adopted, biological line untraceable).

children.name: Susan (Spence) Clarke
children.fact: Hunter's paternal aunt; sister of Dale Jr.; took married name Clarke

children.name: Deanne (Spence) Patton
children.fact: Hunter's paternal aunt; sister of Dale Jr.; took married name Patton

secondary_trees.label: PATERNAL — Henslee line (Frances + Lee Stuart Henslee, Beaumont/Nederland TX)

tree.name: Lee S. + Frances Virginia Henslee
tree.fact: Public cemetery/death indexes strongly support this Beaumont/Nederland couple and their shared Forest Lawn burial cluster. Treating them as Alice Marie Spence's parents remains MEDIUM-confidence until a direct obituary or vital record is attached.

children.name: Lee S. Henslee
children.fact: Public burial index confirms dates, burial at Forest Lawn Memorial Park, Beaumont (Garden of Hope), and spouse Frances Henslee. Local project context treats this as the Lee Stuart Henslee in Alice Marie Spence's line, but the father-of-Alice link remains MEDIUM-confidence until a direct obituary or vital record is attached.

children.name: Alice Marie (Henslee) Spence
children.fact: Hunter's paternal grandmother. Public burial index confirms birth in Rusk, death on 3 Dec 2005, and burial at Forest Lawn Memorial Park, Beaumont (Garden of Hope). Local family context places her as Lee S. and Frances Virginia Henslee's daughter, but that parent-child link remains MEDIUM-confidence until a direct obituary or vital record is attached.

children.name: Don Henslee
children.fact: Frances + Lee Stuart Henslee's son; Hunter's paternal grand-uncle. Lives in Nederland TX. m. Jo Ann.

children.name: Chad Henslee
children.fact: Hunter's first cousin once removed (Don's son)

children.name: Stacy (Henslee) George
children.fact: Hunter's first cousin once removed (Don's daughter, m. George)

children.name: Jennifer (Henslee) Tyler
children.fact: Hunter's first cousin once removed (Don's daughter, m. Tyler)

children.name: Rosalie (Henslee) Stephens
children.fact: Frances's sister; Hunter's paternal great-grand-aunt. Lives in Allen TX. m. Steve Stephens. Per Frances obituary.

secondary_trees.label: MATERNAL-PATERNAL — Teichmüller line (Hans/Johann ~1580 Harz miller → Brunswick → La Grange TX, 6 gens NDB-confirmed)

tree.name: Hans / Johann Teichmüller
tree.fact: EARLIEST documented Teichmüller patrilineal ancestor. Master miller (Mühlenmeister) in the southern Harz mountains. Surname Teichmüller is occupational: 'pond miller'. Per NDB 2016.

children.name: [~2 unnamed Teichmüller generations]
children.fact: NDB notes a gap between earliest Hans/Johann (~1580) and Joachim Andreas (1705). Likely millers / minor Harz officials.

children.name: Joachim Andreas Teichmüller
children.fact: Oberfaktor (chief commercial agent) in Goslar at the foot of the Harz mountains. GND 1154326802. Earliest documented patrilineal Teichmüller before mining-clan generations.

children.name: Wilhelm Ernst Conrad Teichmüller
children.fact: Oberhütteninspekteur (chief smelting works inspector) at Karlshütte near Delligsen, Leinebergland. m. Henriette Christiane Helene Schorkopf (1763-1818) of Uslar. GND 1154326616. August's father.

children.name: August Wilhelm Teichmüller
children.fact: Seconde-Lieutenant in Schwarzen Corps des Majors Olfermann, Brunswick (NOT Prussian) army. m. Charlotte Georgine Elisabeth von Girsewald (1799-1860). 5 children: Wilhelmina, Wilhelm, Hans, Gustav, +1 died young. Per NDB 2016.

children.name: Wilhelmina (Minette) Teichmüller
children.fact: Hans/Gustav's surviving sister (NOT to be confused with Hans's daughter Minette 1871). m. Karl Mollenhauer (Protestant pastor + Superintendent at Bockenem). Per NDB 2016.

children.name: Wilhelm Teichmüller
children.fact: Hans/Gustav's brother. Premier-Lieutenant + Schriftsteller (writer). m. Bertha Kuntzen. Per NDB 2016. POSSIBLE father of pianist Robert Teichmüller (1863-1939).

children.name: Hans Teichmueller
children.dates: 7 Mar 1837 Braunschweig – 17 Feb 1901 La Grange TX
children.fact: Hunter's confirmed great-great-grandfather. TSHA confirms his Braunschweig birth, post-1855 migration to Texas, 1858 Fayette County marriage to Augusta Kellner, five children, and later legal/judicial career in Fayette County. This pass does not extend Kellner parentage beyond Augusta.

children.name: May Teichmueller
children.fact: m. Edward Mattingly Sr. 1 Sept 1894; mother of Leroy Teichmuller Mattingly (Hunter's great-grandfather); Hans's other daughter

children.name: Minette Teichmueller (Pohl)
children.fact: Texas muralist; New Deal Art Registry confirms she painted 'The Law--Texas Rangers' for the Smithville post office in 1940. Often identified as Hans and Augusta's daughter, but that parent-child link still needs a direct census, cemetery, or vital-record citation.

children.name: [5th child of Hans]
children.fact: Lotto 1902 confirms 5 children but 5th not in TX vital records (pre-1903 records sparse) — UNVERIFIED

children.name: Gustav Teichmüller
children.fact: German philosopher and historian of philosophy; professor at Dorpat (Tartu). NDB confirms he was Hans Teichmueller's brother.

children.name: Anna Teichmüller
children.fact: Composer, pianist, and music teacher; NDB identifies her as Gustav Teichmüller's daughter.

secondary_trees.label: MATERNAL — Lepik / Lepick / Mikeska line (Bohemia 1862 → Kansas 1881 → Floresville TX)

tree.name: Frank Lepik (later 'Lepick')
tree.fact: Hunter's maternal 2x-great-grandfather. EMIGRATED 1881 from Bohemia to Brown County Kansas. Surname 'Lepik' Czech/Bohemian; Americanized to 'Lepick' by next generation. m. Mary Mikeska. 9 children (most stayed Kansas/Arkansas; only Fred Sr went to Texas). WikiTree Lepik-8.

children.name: Fred Charles Lepick Sr.
children.fact: Jennive's father. Born Brown County, Kansas to Bohemian immigrant Frank Lepik + Mary Mikeska. Moved to Wilson Co TX 1910-1920; m. Hilda Boehme ~1920. Lived San Antonio late 1930s+. Buried Floresville City Cemetery (Find a Grave 55217353). WikiTree Lepick-2.

children.name: Jennive Imogene Lepick
children.fact: Shari's mother (Hunter's maternal great-grandmother). m. Leroy Baity Mattingly 18 Dec 1943 at Fort Sill OK. Dietician degree from Our Lady of the Lake College, San Antonio. Find a Grave 36080759. WikiTree Lepick-1.

children.name: Gertrude (Lepick) Hurlburt
children.fact: Jennive's older sister

children.name: Fred Charles Lepick Jr.
children.fact: Jennive's younger brother. Naval Aviator (NOT a banker — Frost Bank 'uncle' oral tradition is debunked).

children.name: Mary A. (Lepick) Blacketer
children.fact: Fred Sr.'s sister; m. Blacketer 1908 Kansas

children.name: Frank T. Lepick
children.fact: Fred Sr.'s sibling (Kansas/Arkansas)

children.name: Tilford Anthony Lepick
children.fact: Fred Sr.'s sibling

children.name: Edward Lepick
children.fact: Fred Sr.'s sibling

children.name: William Henry Lepick
children.fact: Fred Sr.'s sibling

children.name: Joseph Lepick
children.fact: Fred Sr.'s sibling

children.name: Jennie Lepick
children.fact: Fred Sr.'s sibling

children.name: Katherine 'Kate' Lepick
children.fact: Fred Sr.'s sibling

secondary_trees.label: MATERNAL — Boehme line (Herman F. Boehme b. 1863 Lavaca Co TX, Lutheran German-Texan)

tree.name: Herman F. Boehme
tree.dates: 9 Jun 1863 TX – 18 Jun 1900 Shiner, Lavaca Co TX
tree.fact: Hilda Boehme's father (Hunter's maternal 2x-great-grandfather). Lutheran. Buried Sons of Hermann Cemetery. German-Texan family, Lavaca/Wilson County. Hilda's mother UNVERIFIED. Per agent 30 research; PeopleLegacy.

secondary_trees.label: MATERNAL-PATERNAL — Baity line research anchors (North Carolina by 1830 → San Antonio builders by 1910)

tree.name: Baity line research anchors
tree.fact: Visual tree of source-backed Baity anchors only. It does not claim a completed direct lineage, immigrant arrival, or the unproved April 1901 Reeves County purchase.

children.name: Surry County Baity household cluster
children.fact: 1830 Surry County census transcription lists David Baity, George Baity, Isam Baity, and Pleasant Baity as separate heads of household. Regional presence note only; not a proved parent-child chain.

children.name: Isham "Isom" Baity
children.fact: Courtney Baptist Church Cemetery, Yadkin County NC, gives exact dates. Strong Yadkin/Surry Baity anchor; direct bridge to W. A. Baity still needs census/probate/vital proof.

children.name: W. A. Baity + Paralee Baity
children.fact: Bexar County records and San Antonio newspapers document W. A. + Paralee as a real-estate couple by 1910; W. A. was a builder/developer and was listed at 211 Castillo Ave by 1925. Expanding W. A. to William Alexander and Pearl to Paralee remains research-note-only until direct records are cited.

secondary_trees.label: MATERNAL grandfather — David Trifon (adopted-name; biological line untraceable per Hunter)

tree.name: David Trifon
tree.fact: Sharyn Mattingly's first husband (m. ~1968); biological father of Rachel. Surname Trifon is an ADOPTED name — his biological lineage is untraceable. Per Hunter 2026-04-26: 'don't trace that one back.'

secondary_trees.label: DEBUNKED — Frost dynasty (was UNVERIFIED, agent 25 confirmed Lepick line is the actual maternal line)

tree.name: Thomas Claiborne Frost I
tree.fact: FOUNDER of Frost Bank San Antonio (1868). Former Confederate Army Lt Col. Bank still Frost-controlled today.

children.name: T.C. Frost II
children.fact: Son of bank founder T.C. Frost I; Frost Bank president gen 2.

children.name: Thomas Claiborne Frost III
children.fact: Grandson of bank founder. m. Florence Ilse Herff (1906-1984)

children.name: Tom Frost Jr.
children.fact: Frost Bank president 1963-1997. Probable 'Shari's uncle' from Hunter's interview.

children.name: Ilse 'Ita' Herff Frost McNeel
children.fact: Daughter of T.C. Frost III + Florence Herff

children.name: Joseph H. Frost
children.fact: Brother of T.C. Frost II; ran Frost Bank. Confirmed Frost-family lineage.
